reason: Modifying the mount options for the boot partition (/boot/efi) impacts the ability of the system to mount its EFI system partition. An incorrect fstab entry can prevent the system from booting, leading to potential lockout. According to policy, changes to bootloader or boot‑related filesystems are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; potential boot failure; inability to mount EFI partition; system may become unbootable
